records.ety: [L. latens, -entis, p. pr. of latere to lie hid or concealed; cf. Gr. lanqa`nein, E. lethargy: cf. F. latent.]

defs: Not visible or apparent; hidden; concealed; secret; dormant; as, latent springs of action.
quotes: The evils latent in the most promising contrivances are provided for as they arise.

defs: Existing but not presenting symptoms; dormant or developing; -- of disease, especially infectious diseases; as, the latent phase of an infection.
